blocks.body: Mixed Conditionals combine different time frames when the cause belongs to one time and the result belongs to another.

{"level":"B2-C1","intro":"Use Mixed Conditionals when the condition and result belong to different times. Most often, a past cause explains a present result, but the opposite direction is also possible.","rules":[{"label":"PAST CAUSE -> PRESENT RESULT","color":"emerald","text":"The if-clause uses the Past Perfect, and the result uses would + base verb.","example":"If I had gone to bed earlier, I would feel better now."},{"label":"PRESENT CONDITION -> PAST RESULT","color":"blue","text":"The if-clause uses the Past Simple, and the result uses would have + V3.","example":"If she were more organised, she would have finished on time."},{"label":"MEANING FIRST","color":"amber","text":"Mix tenses only when the time contrast really matters.","example":"Different time, clear cause and effect."}]}

{"title":"1. Main patterns","intro":"There are two common mixed conditional patterns.","items":[{"label":"Past -> present","title":"if + past perfect, would + V","subtitle":"If I had studied medicine, I would be a doctor now."},{"label":"Present -> past","title":"if + past, would have + V3","subtitle":"If he were more careful, he wouldn't have made that mistake."},{"label":"Focus","title":"cause and result in different times","subtitle":"One part looks back, the other part looks at now or then."},{"label":"Do not mix for no reason","title":"tense choice follows meaning","subtitle":"Use a mixed form only when the time difference matters."}]}

{"title":"2. How the meaning works","sections":[{"label":"Past decision, present consequence","color":"emerald","description":"The past cause is finished, but its effect is still felt now.","examples":[{"en":"If I had accepted that job, I would live in London now.","ua":"If I had accepted that job, I would live in London now."},{"en":"If we had booked a hotel, we wouldn't be looking for a room now.","ua":"If we had booked a hotel, we wouldn't be looking for a room now."}]},{"label":"Present trait, past mistake","color":"blue","description":"A present condition explains why the past result happened that way.","examples":[{"en":"If she were more confident, she would have asked for help.","ua":"If she were more confident, she would have asked for help."},{"en":"If I were better at maths, I would have understood the report yesterday.","ua":"If I were better at maths, I would have understood the report yesterday."}]},{"label":"When you really need a mixed form","color":"amber","description":"Use a mixed pattern only when a simple second or third conditional would give the wrong time relationship.","examples":[{"en":"If he had taken the course, he would know the system now.","ua":"If he had taken the course, he would know the system now."},{"en":"If they were more prepared, they would have reacted faster.","ua":"If they were more prepared, they would have reacted faster."}]}]}

{"title":"3. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Using the Third Conditional when the result is present.","wrong":"If I had gone to bed earlier, I would have felt better now.","right":"If I had gone to bed earlier, I would feel better now.","hint":"A present result needs would + base verb."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Mixing times without a real meaning difference.","wrong":"If it had rained, we would play inside.","right":"If it rained, we would play inside. / If it had rained, we would have played inside.","hint":"Choose the form that truly matches the time of the condition and the result."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Forgetting that only one part changes time.","wrong":"If she had studied more, she would have a better mark now and yesterday.","right":"If she had studied more, she would have a better mark now.","hint":"A mixed conditional needs a clear time focus: either a present result or a past result."}]}

{"title":"4. Quick rule","items":["Past cause -> present result: if + past perfect, would + V.","Present condition -> past result: if + past, would have + V3.","Use a mixed form only when the condition and result truly belong to different times.","Decide the time logic first, then choose the form."]}
